# Software Requirements Specification (SRS)
## Property Management System
**Version:** 1.5  
**Date:** April 28, 2026  
**Prepared For:** Property Owner / Super Admin  
**Technology Stack:** Laravel (Web & Backend), MySQL (Database)

## 1. Introduction

### 1.1 Purpose
This document defines the complete software requirements for a **web-based Property Management System (PMS)**. The system will enable the property owner and their team to manage the entire lifecycle of multiple properties — from purchase through rental operations to eventual sale — while also providing a self-service portal for tenants.

### 1.2 Scope
The system covers:
- Property acquisition and purchase tracking
- Co-ownership and investor management
- Unit-level rental management
- Tenant onboarding with KYC document collection
- Lease lifecycle management
- **Security deposit collection, deduction, and refund tracking** *(v1.1)*
- Manual rent payment recording with **partial / instalment payment support** *(v1.2)*
- **Digital rent agreement creation, e-signature collection, and notarized agreement storage** *(v1.3)*
- **Two-factor authentication (2FA) via Email OTP and WhatsApp OTP for Super Admin and Manager** *(v1.4)*
- **Rent Return processing for early-vacating tenants with pro-rata calculation and optional ledger posting** *(v1.5)*
- Maintenance request tracking
- Property sales process tracking
- Financial income/expense ledger per property
- Automated alerts via Email and SMS/WhatsApp
- Role-based access for Admins, Managers, Owners, and Tenants

### 1.3 Definitions & Acronyms

| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| SRS | Software Requirements Specification |
| PMS | Property Management System |
| KYC | Know Your Customer (identity verification) |
| EMI | Equated Monthly Installment |
| Super Admin | The primary owner with full system access |
| Unit | A rentable space within a property (flat, shop, floor) |
| Tenant | A person or entity renting a unit |
| Manager | Staff assigned to manage specific properties |
| Security Deposit | Refundable amount collected from tenant at lease start |
| Rent Ledger Entry | The monthly rent demand record for a lease, tracking total due and payment status |
| Instalment | A single partial payment made against a monthly rent ledger entry |
| Arrears | Unpaid balance carried forward from a prior month into the current month |
| Digital Agreement | A system-generated rent agreement sent to the tenant for online review and e-signature |
| E-Signature | A digital representation of the tenant's acceptance, captured via draw, type, or image upload |
| Notarized Agreement | The offline, government-approved physical rent agreement uploaded as a scanned document |
| Agreement Link | A unique, time-limited URL sent to the tenant to access and sign the digital agreement |
| 2FA | Two-Factor Authentication — a second verification step required after password login |
| OTP | One-Time Password — a time-limited numeric code sent to the user's email or WhatsApp |
| Backup Code | A single-use recovery code generated at 2FA setup, used when the primary 2FA channel is unavailable |
| TOTP | Time-based One-Time Password — not used in v1.4; reserved for future authenticator app support |
| Rent Return | A formal process to calculate and settle the refund or adjustment due to a tenant who vacates a unit before the end of their paid rental period |
| Vacation Date | The date on which the tenant physically hands back the unit and vacates |
| Daily Rent Rate | Monthly rent ÷ number of days in the billing month, used for pro-rata Rent Return calculation |
| Pro-rata Refund | The refund amount calculated for the unused days remaining in a paid rental period after early vacation |

---

## 2. Overall Description

### 2.1 Product Perspective
A standalone, web-based application built on **Laravel** (PHP framework) with a MySQL database. The system is accessible via modern browsers and requires no mobile application at this stage.

### 2.2 Property Types Supported
- Residential (apartments / houses)
- Commercial (offices / shops)
- Mixed-use properties
- Land / Plots

### 2.3 Scale
- Current: 1–10 properties
- Each property may have one or multiple rentable units
- System must be architected to scale to 50+ properties in future

### 2.4 Property Lifecycle Stages
Every property moves through the following stages (tracked in the system):

```
Under Construction → Ready → Rented → Vacant → For Sale → Sold
```

Transitions between stages are logged with date and the user who triggered the change.

---

## 3. User Roles & Permissions

The system supports four distinct roles with strictly enforced access control.

### 3.1 Super Admin
- Full access to all modules, all properties, and all data
- Can create and manage all user accounts and role assignments
- Can assign owners and managers to properties
- Can configure system-wide settings
- Can create, edit, and delete agreement templates
- Can view, download, and manage all digital and notarized agreements
- Subject to mandatory 2FA on every login (Email OTP or WhatsApp OTP)
- Can manage 2FA settings for all user accounts (reset, unlock, regenerate backup codes)

### 3.2 Property Manager
- Access limited to properties explicitly assigned to them
- Can manage tenants, leases, units, and maintenance requests for assigned properties
- Can record rent payments and instalments, and manage deposit transactions
- Can send digital rent agreements to tenants and view agreement status (Pending / Viewed / Signed)
- Can create and edit agreement templates (cannot delete templates)
- Can download signed agreements and upload notarized agreements for assigned properties
- Cannot view financial data of other properties
- Cannot manage ownership or co-owner equity
- Subject to mandatory 2FA on every login (Email OTP or WhatsApp OTP)

### 3.3 Owner
- Read-only access to properties they own (or co-own)
- Can view: property details, tenant list, lease summaries, income/expense ledger, deposit summary, agreement status, and their ownership share
- Cannot edit any data

### 3.4 Tenant
- Access to their own self-service portal only
- Can view: their lease details, rent payment history, instalment receipts, deposit status, and deductions
- Can upload KYC documents
- Can submit and track maintenance requests
- Can open the digital agreement via the shareable link, review, and sign it (without requiring portal login)
- Can download their signed agreement from the portal after signing

---

## 4. Functional Requirements

---

### 4.1 Authentication & User Management

**FR-AUTH-01:** The system shall support email/password-based login for all user roles. Super Admin and Property Manager accounts additionally require Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) on every login — see Section 4.1a.

**FR-AUTH-02:** Passwords must be stored hashed (bcrypt). A "Forgot Password" flow via email reset link is required.

**FR-AUTH-03:** The Super Admin can create, edit, deactivate, and delete user accounts.

**FR-AUTH-04:** Each user account is assigned exactly one role. Role changes are logged.

**FR-AUTH-05:** Sessions shall expire after a configurable idle period (default: 60 minutes).

**FR-AUTH-06:** Tenants receive an invitation email with a one-time setup link to create their portal password when onboarded by a manager.

---

### 4.1a Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) *(New — v1.4)*

This section defines the requirements for Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) for the PMS. 2FA adds a mandatory second verification step after password login, protecting sensitive financial, KYC, and tenant data from unauthorised access even in the event of a compromised password.

---

**FR-2FA-01: Scope & Mandatory Enforcement**

2FA is **mandatory** for the following roles and cannot be disabled by the user:
- **Super Admin** — enforced at all times, no exceptions
- **Property Manager** — enforced at all times, no exceptions

2FA is **not applicable** to the following roles:
- **Owner** — read-only access; 2FA may be added in a future version
- **Tenant** — self-service portal only; not subject to 2FA

The system shall block Super Admin and Property Manager logins entirely until 2FA setup is completed on their account. If a Manager account is created by the Super Admin and 2FA has not yet been configured, the system shall redirect the user to 2FA setup immediately upon first login before granting any access.

---

**FR-2FA-02: Supported 2FA Methods**

The system shall support the following two OTP delivery channels. The user selects their preferred method during setup and may change it later from account settings:

- **Email OTP** — a 6-digit numeric OTP is sent to the user's registered email address via the system's configured email provider (SMTP / Mailgun / SendGrid)
- **WhatsApp OTP** — a 6-digit numeric OTP is sent to the user's registered mobile number via the configured WhatsApp provider (Twilio / MSG91 / Interakt)

Both methods use the same OTP generation and validation logic. The user can switch their preferred method at any time from account settings, subject to re-verification with the current method before the switch is saved.

---

**FR-2FA-03: 2FA Setup Flow**

When a Super Admin or Manager logs in for the first time, or when 2FA has been reset by an admin, the system shall walk the user through a one-time setup:

1. User selects their preferred OTP delivery method (Email or WhatsApp)
2. System sends a test OTP to the selected channel
3. User enters the test OTP to confirm the channel is working
4. System generates **8 single-use backup codes** and displays them on screen
5. User must acknowledge that they have saved the backup codes (mandatory checkbox) before proceeding
6. 2FA is marked as configured on the account; setup is complete

Backup codes are displayed only once. The user may regenerate them later from account security settings, which immediately invalidates all previously issued backup codes.

---

**FR-2FA-04: Login Flow with 2FA**

After email/password credentials are verified successfully, the system shall:

1. Display a 2FA verification screen (user cannot access any part of the system until this step is passed)
2. Automatically send an OTP to the user's configured channel (Email or WhatsApp)
3. Show the destination partially masked (e.g., `j***@gmail.com` or `+91 98***1234`) so the user knows where to look
4. Provide a "Resend OTP" option, available after a 30-second cooldown, with a maximum of 3 resend attempts per login session
5. Accept the OTP entered by the user and validate it

On successful OTP validation → session starts and user is granted access.
On failure → see FR-2FA-06 for lockout rules.

There is no trusted device option. 2FA is required on **every login session** without exception.

---

**FR-2FA-05: OTP Specifications**

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Format | 6-digit numeric code |
| Validity period | 10 minutes from generation |
| Single use | Yes — OTP is invalidated immediately after first successful use |
| Resend cooldown | 30 seconds between resend requests |
| Max resend attempts | 3 per login session |
| Delivery retry | If delivery fails (e.g. WhatsApp provider error), system automatically falls back to Email OTP and notifies the user |

---

**FR-2FA-06: Brute Force Protection & Lockout**

The system shall enforce the following protections against OTP brute-forcing:

- After **5 consecutive failed OTP attempts** in a single login session, the account is temporarily locked for **15 minutes**
- During lockout, no further OTP attempts are accepted and no new OTPs are sent
- A lockout notification is sent to the user via both Email and WhatsApp (if configured) informing them of the lockout and its duration
- After 15 minutes, the user may attempt login again from the beginning (credentials + new OTP)
- If a lockout occurs **3 times within 24 hours**, the account is escalated to a **hard lock** requiring manual Super Admin intervention to unlock
- All lockout events are recorded in the audit log with timestamp and IP address
- For a Super Admin account hard lock, the system shall send an alert to a pre-configured recovery email address (set in system configuration)

---

**FR-2FA-07: Backup Code Recovery**

If a user cannot receive their OTP (e.g. lost phone, email inaccessible), they may use a backup code to complete login:

- The login screen shall include a clearly labelled "Use a backup code instead" option
- Each backup code is 10 characters (alphanumeric), single-use, and invalidated immediately after use
- The system shall display a warning when the user has **2 or fewer backup codes remaining**, prompting them to regenerate
- When a backup code is used, a notification is sent to the user via their registered email and WhatsApp, alerting them that a backup code was consumed
- Backup code usage is recorded in the audit log

---

**FR-2FA-08: 2FA Method Change**

A logged-in user may change their preferred OTP delivery method (Email ↔ WhatsApp) from account security settings:

1. User selects the new preferred method
2. System sends a verification OTP to the **new** channel
3. User enters the OTP to confirm the new channel is accessible
4. System also sends a confirmation notification to the **old** channel informing them the 2FA method was changed
5. Change is saved and logged in the audit trail

---

**FR-2FA-09: Admin 2FA Management**

The Super Admin shall have access to a 2FA management panel showing all user accounts with:
- 2FA status per user (Configured / Not Configured / Locked)
- Preferred OTP method
- Date 2FA was first configured
- Date of last successful 2FA login
- Number of backup codes remaining

Available admin actions per user account:
- **Reset 2FA** — clears the user's 2FA configuration and forces them through setup again on next login. This action is logged and a notification is sent to the affected user.
- **Unlock account** — manually unlocks a hard-locked account (FR-2FA-06). Requires the Super Admin to enter their own 2FA OTP to confirm the action.
- **Regenerate backup codes** — generates a fresh set of 8 backup codes on behalf of a user (e.g. if the user has lost all codes). The new codes are sent to the user's registered email and WhatsApp.

The Super Admin cannot view or copy a user's existing backup codes — they can only regenerate them.

---

**FR-2FA-10: 2FA Audit Trail**

All 2FA-related events shall be recorded in the audit log with timestamp, user ID, IP address, and device/browser user agent:

| Event | Logged |
|-------|--------|
| 2FA setup completed | Yes |
| Successful 2FA login | Yes |
| Failed OTP attempt | Yes (with attempt count) |
| Account locked (soft) | Yes |
| Account locked (hard) | Yes |
| Backup code used | Yes (code index, not the code value) |
| Backup codes regenerated | Yes (by user or by admin) |
| 2FA method changed | Yes (old and new method) |
| 2FA reset by admin | Yes (admin user ID recorded) |
| Account unlocked by admin | Yes (admin user ID recorded) |

The 2FA audit trail is read-only. It is accessible to Super Admin only and is separate from the general system audit log for clarity.

---

### 4.2 Property Management

**FR-PROP-01:** The system shall allow the Super Admin and Manager to create a property record with the following details:
- Property name / title
- Property type (Residential / Commercial / Mixed-use / Land)
- Address (Street, City, State, PIN, Country)
- Total area (sq. ft. / sq. m.)
- Current lifecycle stage
- Description / notes
- Cover photo and multiple property photos (file upload)

**FR-PROP-02:** The system shall display a property listing page with filters by type, lifecycle stage, and assigned manager.

**FR-PROP-03:** Each property shall have a dedicated detail page showing all associated data: purchase info, units, tenants, owners, financials, documents, and activity log.

**FR-PROP-04:** The Super Admin can transition a property's lifecycle stage. Each transition is timestamped and logged.

**FR-PROP-05:** Properties can be soft-deleted (archived) but not permanently deleted to preserve historical records.

---

### 4.3 Property Purchase Lifecycle

**FR-PUR-01:** For each property, the system shall allow recording of purchase information:
- Purchase price
- Purchase date
- Stamp duty and registration charges
- Other acquisition costs (brokerage, legal fees, etc.)
- Total cost of acquisition (auto-calculated)
- Seller name and contact details
- Purchase notes / remarks

**FR-PUR-02:** Legal documents related to purchase shall be uploadable and stored against the property (e.g., Sale Deed, Title Certificate, NOC). Each document has a name, type, upload date, and uploaded-by reference.

**FR-PUR-03:** **Mortgage / Loan Tracking:** If the property is financed, the system shall record:
- Lender name (bank / NBFC)
- Loan amount
- Interest rate (fixed/floating)
- Loan start date and tenure (months)
- EMI amount
- EMI due date (day of month)
- EMI payment log: each EMI entry records amount paid, date paid, principal component, interest component, and outstanding balance

**FR-PUR-04:** The system shall display a loan summary showing: total EMIs paid, total interest paid to date, outstanding principal, and remaining tenure.

**FR-PUR-05:** **Co-owner Equity Tracking:** Each property can have multiple owners. For each owner, the system records:
- Owner (linked to a user account or stored as a named contact)
- Ownership percentage (must total 100% across all owners)
- Capital contribution amount
- Date of contribution
- Notes

**FR-PUR-06:** The system shall prevent saving ownership records if the total percentage across all owners does not equal 100%.

---

### 4.4 Property Sales Lifecycle

**FR-SALE-01:** When a property is marked "For Sale", the system shall allow creation of a Sales record with:
- Listing date
- Asking price
- Broker/agent name and contact (optional)
- Listing notes

**FR-SALE-02:** **Lead & Offer Tracking:** The system shall allow logging of prospective buyer interactions:
- Buyer name and contact
- Inquiry date
- Offer amount
- Offer date
- Status (Enquiry / Offer Made / Negotiation / Accepted / Rejected / Lapsed)
- Notes per interaction

**FR-SALE-03:** **Sale Closure:** Once a sale is finalized, the system records:
- Final sale price
- Sale date
- Buyer name and contact
- Sale deed upload
- Broker commission (if any)
- Other closing costs

**FR-SALE-04:** The system shall auto-calculate and display:
- Total cost of acquisition (from FR-PUR-01)
- Total sale proceeds (net of commission and costs)
- Gross profit / loss
- Per-owner profit/loss share (based on ownership percentage)

**FR-SALE-05:** Upon sale completion, the property lifecycle stage is automatically updated to "Sold" and the property becomes read-only (no new tenants or leases can be added).

---

### 4.5 Unit Management

**FR-UNIT-01:** A property can have one or multiple rentable units. Each unit record contains:
- Unit name / number (e.g., Flat 101, Shop-A, Ground Floor)
- Unit type (Residential / Commercial / Other)
- Floor number (optional)
- Area (sq. ft.)
- Current status: Vacant / Occupied / Under Maintenance
- Monthly rent amount (default/expected)
- Description / notes

**FR-UNIT-02:** The property detail page shall show all units with their current occupancy status in a summary view.

**FR-UNIT-03:** A unit can only have one active lease/tenant at a time. The system shall enforce this constraint.

**FR-UNIT-04:** Unit status is automatically updated when a lease is created (→ Occupied) or ended (→ Vacant).

---

### 4.6 Tenant Management & KYC

**FR-TEN-01:** A tenant record shall include:
- Full name
- Date of birth
- Gender
- Primary phone number
- Alternate phone number (optional)
- Email address
- Permanent address
- Emergency contact name and phone
- Occupation / employer name (optional)
- Notes

**FR-TEN-02:** Tenants are independent of any specific unit/property — the same tenant can be re-assigned to a new unit in future (tenant history is preserved).

**FR-TEN-03:** **KYC Document Storage:** The following document types shall be supported for upload against a tenant:
- Aadhaar Card (front & back)
- PAN Card
- Passport (if applicable)
- Visa (if applicable)
- Employment / Income Proof (salary slip, IT return, etc.)
- Signed Rental Agreement copy
- Police Verification Document
- Any other document (with custom label)

**FR-TEN-04:** Each KYC document record stores:
- Document type
- File upload (PDF or image)
- Upload date
- Uploaded by (tenant self-upload or staff upload)
- Notes

**FR-TEN-05:** No approval workflow is required for KYC documents. Documents are stored as-is for reference.

**FR-TEN-06:** The system shall display a KYC completeness indicator per tenant, showing which documents have been uploaded and which are pending (based on a configurable required document checklist per property type).

**FR-TEN-07:** Managers and Super Admin can view all tenant records. Owners see tenant names only (no personal/KYC details). Tenants can view and upload their own documents only.

---

### 4.7 Lease Management

**FR-LEASE-01:** A lease links one tenant to one unit for a defined period. Lease details include:
- Tenant (linked)
- Unit (linked)
- Lease start date
- Lease end date
- Monthly rent amount
- Security deposit amount *(managed in detail via Section 4.7a)*
- Deposit payment date and receipt reference
- Rent due day of month (e.g., 5th of every month)
- Grace period (days before late fee applies)
- Late fee amount or percentage
- Lease type (Fixed term / Month-to-month)
- Signed lease agreement upload
- Special terms / notes

**FR-LEASE-02:** Lease statuses: Draft / Active / Expiring Soon / Expired / Terminated.

**FR-LEASE-03:** The system shall automatically flag leases as "Expiring Soon" when the end date is within a configurable threshold (default: 30 days).

**FR-LEASE-04:** A lease can be renewed by creating a new lease record linked to the same tenant and unit (with updated dates and rent).

**FR-LEASE-05:** Early termination shall be recorded with: termination date, reason, and any penalties.

**FR-LEASE-06:** Security deposit refund is managed through the dedicated Deposit Management module (Section 4.7a). The lease record shall display a summary of deposit status and refund outcome.

---

### 4.7a Security Deposit Management *(New — v1.1)*

This module manages the full lifecycle of security deposits: collection, deductions during tenancy, and final refund upon vacation.

---

**FR-DEP-01: Deposit Collection Recording**

When a lease is created or activated, the system shall allow recording of the security deposit collection as a dedicated transaction:
- Amount collected
- Date collected
- Payment mode (Cash / Bank Transfer / Cheque / UPI / Other)
- Reference number (cheque no., UTR, etc.)
- Notes
- Recorded by (staff user)

A PDF receipt shall be auto-generated upon recording and shall be downloadable by both staff and the tenant via the portal.

---

**FR-DEP-02: Deposit Sub-Ledger**

Each lease shall maintain a deposit sub-ledger showing a complete transaction history:
- Original amount collected
- All deduction entries (itemized)
- All partial refund entries (if any)
- Current refundable balance (auto-calculated: collected − deductions − refunds issued)
- Final status

---

**FR-DEP-03: Deposit Deductions**

Staff (Manager / Super Admin) can record deductions against a deposit at any point during or after the tenancy:
- Deduction amount
- Category: Unpaid Rent / Property Damage / Cleaning / Penalty / Other
- Description / justification (required)
- Supporting document or photo upload (optional)
- Date of deduction
- Recorded by (staff user)

Multiple deductions can be recorded against a single lease deposit. Each deduction is timestamped and logged.

---

**FR-DEP-04: Deposit Refund Recording**

Upon lease termination or expiry, the system shall allow recording of the deposit refund:
- Refund amount (auto-suggested as: deposit collected − total deductions; editable by staff)
- Refund date
- Payment mode and reference number
- Notes
- Recorded by (staff user)

The system shall support both **full refund** and **partial refund**. The remaining balance after a partial refund remains tracked in the sub-ledger.

---

**FR-DEP-05: Deposit Status Tracking**

Each lease deposit shall have a clear status that is automatically maintained:

```
Pending Collection → Collected → Partially Refunded → Fully Refunded → Forfeited
```

- **Pending Collection:** Lease created but deposit payment not yet recorded.
- **Collected:** Deposit received; no or partial deductions; no refund issued yet.
- **Partially Refunded:** A refund has been issued but balance remains.
- **Fully Refunded:** Full refundable balance has been returned to tenant.
- **Forfeited:** Entire deposit retained (e.g., due to lease breach); must be explicitly marked by staff with a reason.

---

**FR-DEP-06: Tenant Portal View**

Tenants shall be able to view the following from their self-service portal:
- Security deposit amount collected
- Itemized list of any deductions (amount, category, reason, date)
- Refund(s) issued (amount, date, mode)
- Current refundable balance
- Deposit status

Tenants cannot edit any deposit records.

---

**FR-DEP-07: Deposit Report**

A dedicated **Deposit Summary Report** shall be available under the Financial Reports section (see FR-FIN-04), showing across all properties (filterable by property, date range, and status):

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Property / Unit | Property and unit name |
| Tenant | Tenant name |
| Lease Period | Lease start and end date |
| Deposit Collected | Original deposit amount |
| Total Deductions | Sum of all deductions |
| Refund Issued | Total refunded so far |
| Balance Held | Current outstanding balance |
| Status | Current deposit status |
| Days Since Vacation | Days since lease ended (for overdue refund tracking) |

The report shall flag in red any deposit where refund is pending more than a configurable number of days after lease end (default: 30 days).

The report shall be exportable to PDF and CSV.

---

**FR-DEP-08: Deposit Forfeiture**

If the entire deposit is to be retained (e.g., due to tenant lease breach or abandonment), staff can mark the deposit as **Forfeited**:
- Forfeiture reason (required)
- Date of forfeiture
- Supporting notes

Forfeited deposit amounts shall be recorded as income in the property's financial ledger automatically.

---

**FR-DEP-09: Deposit Adjustment (Top-up)**

If a lease is renewed with a higher rent, staff may record a deposit top-up:
- Additional amount collected
- Date and payment reference
- The deposit sub-ledger is updated cumulatively

---

### 4.8 Rent & Payment Tracking *(Revised — v1.2)*

This section has been overhauled to support partial and instalment-based rent payments. The core model shift is that a **Rent Ledger Entry** (the monthly demand) is now separate from **Rent Instalments** (individual payments made against that demand). A single ledger entry can have one or many instalment records.

---

**FR-PAY-01:** The system shall use manual payment recording only (no payment gateway integration).

---

**FR-PAY-02: Monthly Rent Ledger**

For each active lease, the system shall auto-generate one **Rent Ledger Entry** per month, based on the rent due date and lease period. Each ledger entry represents the full monthly demand and tracks:
- Lease reference
- Payment month (e.g., April 2026)
- Base rent amount due
- Carried arrears from prior months (auto-populated, read-only)
- Total amount due (base rent + carried arrears, auto-calculated)
- Total amount received (sum of all instalments, auto-calculated)
- Outstanding balance (total due − total received, auto-calculated)
- Status: **Unpaid / Partially Paid / Fully Paid / Overdue**
- Notes

Ledger entry statuses are updated automatically as instalments are recorded.

---

**FR-PAY-03: Instalment Recording**

Staff (Manager / Super Admin) can record one or more payment instalments against any open rent ledger entry. Each instalment record includes:
- Parent ledger entry reference (month + lease)
- Instalment number (auto-incremented per ledger entry, e.g., Instalment 1, 2, 3…)
- Amount paid
- Payment date
- Payment mode (Cash / Bank Transfer / Cheque / UPI / Other)
- Reference number (cheque no., UTR, UPI transaction ID, etc.)
- Late fee charged (if applicable — see FR-PAY-04)
- Notes
- Recorded by (staff user)

There is no upper limit on the number of instalments per ledger entry. All instalments are timestamped and included in the audit log.

---

**FR-PAY-04: Late Fee on Outstanding Balance**

The system shall calculate and suggest a late fee when an instalment is recorded after the grace period has elapsed. Late fee is applied on the **outstanding balance at the time of recording** (not the full monthly amount), so tenants who make partial payments before the grace period are only penalised on the remaining unpaid portion. Staff may override or waive the suggested late fee and must enter a reason for any waiver.

---

**FR-PAY-05: Instalment Receipts**

Each instalment shall generate a downloadable PDF receipt showing:
- Property and unit name
- Tenant name and lease period
- Payment month
- Instalment number
- Amount paid in this instalment
- Late fee charged (if any)
- Cumulative amount paid to date for the month
- Outstanding balance remaining for the month
- Payment mode and reference number
- Recorded by and timestamp

Tenants can download all their instalment receipts from the self-service portal.

---

**FR-PAY-06: Arrears Carry-Forward**

If a rent ledger entry's outstanding balance is greater than zero at the end of the payment month, the system shall automatically carry forward the unpaid balance as **arrears** into the following month's ledger entry. The next month's ledger entry shall display:
- Current month base rent
- Arrears brought forward (itemised with the source month)
- Total amount due

Arrears accumulate month over month until fully cleared. The system shall maintain a full arrears history per lease showing which months had shortfalls and how they were eventually settled.

---

**FR-PAY-07: Overpayment Handling**

If the total instalments recorded for a month exceed the total amount due, the system shall:
- Mark the ledger entry as Fully Paid
- Record the excess as a **credit balance** against the lease
- Automatically apply the credit balance to the next month's ledger entry, reducing the amount due
- Display the credit clearly on the next month's ledger entry with a source reference

Staff may also manually refund the overpaid amount to the tenant and record the refund transaction.

---

**FR-PAY-08: Payment Correction Log**

Staff may correct an instalment's payment mode or reference number after recording, without deleting the original entry. Corrections are logged in the audit trail showing the original value, the corrected value, who made the change, and when. Amount corrections are not permitted after recording — if an amount error occurs, staff must void the instalment (with a reason) and re-record.

---

**FR-PAY-09: Rent Dashboard**

The rent dashboard for managers and admins shall display the following views, filterable by property and unit:

- **Upcoming dues** — all ledger entries due in the next 30 days with total expected
- **Partially paid** — leases where at least one instalment has been received but the month is not fully settled
- **Overdue** — ledger entries past the grace period with an outstanding balance
- **Arrears tracker** — tenants carrying forward unpaid balances from prior months
- **Recently recorded** — instalments recorded in the last 7 days

---

**FR-PAY-10: Payment History View**

Each lease shall have a dedicated payment history page showing all months in the lease period as a timeline. For each month:
- Ledger entry status (colour-coded: Fully Paid / Partially Paid / Unpaid / Overdue)
- All instalments recorded (amount, date, mode, receipt download link)
- Arrears brought forward (if any)
- Late fees charged
- Net outstanding balance

This view is accessible to Managers, Super Admin, and the Tenant (read-only).

---

### 4.8a Rent Return *(New — v1.5)*

This module handles the formal process of calculating and settling the rent adjustment due to a tenant who vacates a unit before the end of their paid rental period, or when a full month's rent was collected but the tenant occupied the unit for only a portion of that month.

**Illustrative Example:**
- Tenant A's lease runs April 1 – April 30. Full April rent of ₹25,000 was collected.
- Tenant A vacates on April 10.
- From April 11, the same unit is rented to Tenant B.
- The system should allow the admin to process a Rent Return for Tenant A covering April 11–30 (20 unused days).

A Rent Return can be initiated at any time — it is independent of whether a new tenant has been assigned to the unit.

---

**FR-RENTRET-01: Rent Return Trigger**

A Rent Return can be initiated by a Manager or Super Admin in either of the following situations:

- **Early vacation:** The tenant vacates before their lease end date and rent has already been collected for days beyond the vacation date.
- **Overpaid month:** The tenant paid a full month's rent but occupied the unit for fewer days than the billing period (e.g. mid-month departure with full month collected).

The system shall display a **"Process Rent Return"** action on the lease detail page whenever a lease has been terminated or marked as vacated and the system detects a potential overpayment based on the vacation date vs. the last paid-through date.

---

**FR-RENTRET-02: Rent Return Calculation**

Upon initiating a Rent Return, the system shall auto-calculate and display a suggested return amount using the following logic:

```
Daily Rent Rate  = Monthly Rent ÷ Total Days in Billing Month
Unused Days      = Last Paid-Through Date − Vacation Date
Suggested Return = Daily Rent Rate × Unused Days
```

**Calculation inputs (auto-populated, editable by admin):**
- Vacation date (pulled from lease termination record; editable)
- Last paid-through date (pulled from rent ledger; editable)
- Monthly rent amount (pulled from lease; editable)
- Billing month day count (auto-determined from the month; editable)

The admin may override the suggested return amount before confirming. If the admin changes the amount, they must enter a reason for the override. Both the system-suggested amount and the final confirmed amount are stored for audit purposes.

**Special cases handled:**

| Scenario | System Behaviour |
|----------|-----------------|
| Tenant vacates on exact lease end date, full month paid | Suggested return = ₹0; admin may still initiate if needed |
| Tenant vacates mid-month, only used days were charged (no overpayment) | System flags: "No overpayment detected" and suggests ₹0; admin can still override |
| Tenant has outstanding arrears | System displays outstanding balance alongside the return amount; admin decides how to net them (see FR-RENTRET-04) |
| Tenant has active security deposit | Rent Return is processed separately from deposit refund; both are shown on the lease closure summary |

---

**FR-RENTRET-03: Rent Return Record**

Each Rent Return record shall store:
- Linked lease and tenant
- Linked unit and property
- Vacation date
- Last paid-through date
- Billing month and daily rate used
- Unused days (calculated)
- System-suggested return amount
- Admin-confirmed return amount (may differ if overridden)
- Override reason (if amount was changed)
- Status (see FR-RENTRET-05)
- Settlement method and details (see FR-RENTRET-04)
- Financial ledger posting status (see FR-RENTRET-06)
- Notes
- Initiated by (staff user) and initiated at (timestamp)
- Processed by (staff user) and processed at (timestamp)

---

**FR-RENTRET-04: Settlement — Admin Decides Case by Case**

The system does not enforce a fixed settlement method. When confirming a Rent Return, the admin selects the settlement approach from the following options:

- **Cash refund** — admin records the refund paid to the tenant (amount, date, payment mode, reference number)
- **Adjust against arrears** — the return amount is applied against any outstanding rent dues or late fees on the same lease; any remaining balance after adjustment is either refunded or waived
- **Adjust against deposit** — the return amount is credited toward the security deposit refund calculation (reduces the net deposit refundable amount or increases it, depending on direction)
- **Write-off / Waived** — no money changes hands; admin records a reason (e.g. tenant agreement, goodwill)
- **Pending / TBD** — admin saves the Rent Return record without settling it immediately; settlement can be completed later

All settlement entries are timestamped, recorded by the staff user, and included in the audit trail.

---

**FR-RENTRET-05: Rent Return Status Flow**

Each Rent Return record progresses through the following statuses:

```
Initiated → Confirmed → Settled
                      → Waived
                      → Pending Settlement
```

- **Initiated:** Admin has started the Rent Return process; amount not yet confirmed
- **Confirmed:** Admin has confirmed the return amount (with or without override); settlement not yet recorded
- **Settled:** Settlement has been recorded (refund paid, adjusted, or applied)
- **Waived:** Admin has recorded the return as waived; no money changes hands
- **Pending Settlement:** Confirmed but settlement deferred; appears in the pending actions dashboard

All status transitions are timestamped and logged in the audit trail.

---

**FR-RENTRET-06: Optional Financial Ledger Posting**

When confirming or settling a Rent Return, the admin is presented with a checkbox:

**"Post this Rent Return to the property financial ledger"**

- If checked: the system records a **negative income entry** (rent reversal) in the property ledger for the return amount, with the Rent Return record as the source reference. Category: *Rent Return / Reversal*.
- If unchecked: the Rent Return is recorded only on the lease record and does not affect the property's income ledger.

This makes ledger posting an explicit, deliberate admin decision rather than automatic, giving the property owner full control over how returns are reflected in financial reports.

The ledger posting choice and its outcome are stored on the Rent Return record and included in the audit trail.

---

**FR-RENTRET-07: Vacancy Gap Protection**

When a new lease (Tenant B) is being created for a unit that had a recent Tenant A, the system shall:

- Display a notice on the new lease creation form: *"A previous tenant vacated this unit on [date]. A Rent Return for [Tenant A name] is [Pending / Not Yet Initiated]. You may proceed with this lease without processing it first."*
- Provide a quick-action link to initiate the Rent Return for Tenant A directly from the new lease creation screen, without blocking the workflow
- Ensure the billing periods of Tenant A and Tenant B do not overlap — the system shall validate that Tenant B's lease start date is on or after Tenant A's vacation date and show an error if there is a date conflict

This ensures visibility without creating a hard dependency between the two leases.

---

**FR-RENTRET-08: Tenant Portal Visibility**

The tenant shall be able to view their Rent Return record from the self-service portal:

- Rent Return status (Initiated / Confirmed / Settled / Waived / Pending)
- Vacation date and last paid-through date
- Unused days and calculated return amount
- Confirmed return amount (and note if it was overridden)
- Settlement details (method, date, amount — if settled)

Tenants cannot edit or initiate a Rent Return. They can download a Rent Return summary PDF once the record is in Confirmed or Settled status.

---

**FR-RENTRET-09: Rent Return Report**

A **Rent Return Report** shall be available under the Financial Reports section (FR-FIN-04), showing across all properties (filterable by property, date range, and status):

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Property / Unit | Property and unit name |
| Tenant | Tenant name |
| Vacation Date | Date tenant vacated |
| Unused Days | Days for which return was calculated |
| Suggested Amount | System-calculated return amount |
| Confirmed Amount | Admin-confirmed amount |
| Status | Current Rent Return status |
| Settlement Method | How it was settled (if applicable) |
| Ledger Posted | Whether it was posted to financial ledger |

The report shall flag in amber any Rent Return records in **Pending Settlement** status older than a configurable number of days (default: 7 days). The report shall be exportable to PDF and CSV.

---

### 4.9 Maintenance Requests

**FR-MAINT-01:** Both tenants (via portal) and managers/admins (on behalf of a tenant) can create maintenance requests.

**FR-MAINT-02:** A maintenance request record includes:
- Title / short description
- Category (Plumbing / Electrical / Carpentry / Cleaning / Other)
- Priority (Low / Medium / High / Urgent)
- Detailed description
- Photo attachments (optional, up to 5 images)
- Linked unit and property
- Submitted by (tenant or staff)
- Submission date

**FR-MAINT-03:** Request statuses: Open / In Progress / Pending Tenant Confirmation / Resolved / Closed / Rejected.

**FR-MAINT-04:** Staff can update status, add internal notes, record the vendor/contractor used, and record the cost of repair.

**FR-MAINT-05:** Tenants receive a notification (email/WhatsApp) when their request status changes.

**FR-MAINT-06:** Maintenance costs recorded against a request are optionally linkable to the property's expense ledger.

---

### 4.10 Owner & Co-Ownership Management

**FR-OWN-01:** Each property can have multiple owners. Owners are either linked to existing user accounts or stored as named contacts (for passive investors without system access).

**FR-OWN-02:** Ownership percentage is stored per owner per property. Total must equal 100%.

**FR-OWN-03:** Capital contribution history is tracked per owner (amount, date, purpose/round, notes).

**FR-OWN-04:** The system shall calculate and display each owner's proportional share of:
- Total acquisition cost
- Total rental income received (cumulative)
- Total expenses incurred (cumulative)
- Net income (income minus expenses)
- Profit/loss upon sale

**FR-OWN-05:** Ownership records are timestamped and changes are logged (e.g., ownership transfer or buyout).

---

### 4.11 Financial Ledger & Reporting

**FR-FIN-01:** Each property maintains an independent income and expense ledger.

**FR-FIN-02:** **Income entries** are auto-created from recorded rent payments. Additional income types can be added manually (e.g., parking charges, amenity fees). Forfeited deposit amounts (FR-DEP-08) are also auto-recorded as income.

**FR-FIN-03:** **Expense entries** can be recorded manually with:
- Date
- Category (Maintenance / Loan EMI / Property Tax / Insurance / Management Fee / Utility / Other)
- Amount
- Vendor / payee name
- Invoice/receipt upload
- Notes

**FR-FIN-04:** The system shall provide the following reports:
- **Property P&L Summary:** Total income vs total expenses vs net income for a selected date range, per property
- **Rent Collection Report:** Monthly rent due vs collected vs outstanding, per property/unit
- **Expense Report:** Expenses by category for a selected date range, per property
- **Owner Income Statement:** Per owner, their share of income, expenses, and net income
- **Loan Repayment Schedule:** Full EMI schedule with paid/pending status
- **Deposit Summary Report:** *(New — v1.1)* Deposits held, deductions, refunds, balance, and overdue refunds across all properties (see FR-DEP-07)
- **Arrears & Partial Payments Report:** *(New — v1.2)* Per property/unit, shows all leases with outstanding balances, month-wise arrears history, and instalment breakdown. Flags leases where arrears have been carried for more than a configurable number of months (default: 2).
- **Rent Return Report:** *(New — v1.5)* Shows all Rent Return records across properties with vacation dates, unused days, suggested vs confirmed amounts, settlement method, ledger posting status, and flags pending settlements older than a configurable threshold (see FR-RENTRET-09).

**FR-FIN-05:** All reports shall be exportable to PDF and CSV.

---

### 4.12 Notifications & Alerts

**FR-NOTIF-01:** The system shall send automated alerts via **Email** and **SMS/WhatsApp** for the following events:

| Event | Recipients |
|-------|------------|
| Rent due reminder (X days before due date) | Tenant, Manager |
| Rent overdue (after grace period) | Tenant, Manager, Super Admin |
| Lease expiring soon (30 days before) | Manager, Super Admin, Owner |
| Lease expired | Manager, Super Admin |
| KYC document pending / expiring | Tenant, Manager |
| Maintenance request status changed | Tenant |
| EMI due reminder (3 days before) | Super Admin |
| New tenant onboarded (portal invite) | Tenant |
| **Deposit collection pending (X days after lease start)** *(New — v1.1)* | **Manager, Super Admin** |
| **Deposit refund overdue (X days after lease end, refund not recorded)** *(New — v1.1)* | **Manager, Super Admin** |
| **Deposit refund processed** *(New — v1.1)* | **Tenant** |
| **Deposit deduction recorded** *(New — v1.1)* | **Tenant** |
| **Partial payment received (instalment recorded, balance still outstanding)** *(New — v1.2)* | **Tenant, Manager** |
| **Arrears carried forward (unpaid balance moves to new month)** *(New — v1.2)* | **Tenant, Manager, Super Admin** |
| **Instalment receipt generated** *(New — v1.2)* | **Tenant** |
| **Digital agreement sent to tenant** *(New — v1.3)* | **Tenant (via email/WhatsApp), Manager** |
| **Digital agreement viewed by tenant** *(New — v1.3)* | **Manager, Super Admin** |
| **Digital agreement signed by tenant** *(New — v1.3)* | **Tenant (signed PDF copy), Manager, Super Admin** |
| **Notarized agreement upload pending (X days after lease start)** *(New — v1.3)* | **Manager, Super Admin** |
| **OTP sent for 2FA login** *(New — v1.4)* | **User (via Email or WhatsApp)** |
| **Account soft-locked after failed OTP attempts** *(New — v1.4)* | **User (via Email + WhatsApp)** |
| **Account hard-locked (3 soft-locks in 24 hrs)** *(New — v1.4)* | **User, Super Admin (recovery email)** |
| **Backup code used during login** *(New — v1.4)* | **User (via Email + WhatsApp)** |
| **2FA method changed** *(New — v1.4)* | **User (old channel notified)** |
| **2FA reset by admin** *(New — v1.4)* | **Affected user (via Email + WhatsApp)** |
| **Rent Return initiated for tenant** *(New — v1.5)* | **Tenant (via Email + WhatsApp)** |
| **Rent Return settled** *(New — v1.5)* | **Tenant (via Email + WhatsApp)** |
| **Rent Return pending settlement overdue (X days)** *(New — v1.5)* | **Manager, Super Admin** |

**FR-NOTIF-02:** The Super Admin can configure notification triggers: enable/disable each event type, and set the number of days before/after the event for reminders.

**FR-NOTIF-03:** All sent notifications are logged in the system (recipient, channel, timestamp, message preview).

**FR-NOTIF-04:** WhatsApp/SMS integration shall use a third-party provider (e.g., Twilio, MSG91, or Interakt). Email shall use SMTP or a transactional email provider (e.g., Mailgun, SendGrid).

---

### 4.13 Document Management

**FR-DOC-01:** Documents can be attached at three levels: Property level, Tenant/KYC level, and Lease level.

**FR-DOC-02:** Supported file formats: PDF, JPG, PNG, DOCX. Maximum file size per upload: 10 MB.

**FR-DOC-03:** All documents are stored in a cloud-compatible file storage system (e.g., AWS S3 or local disk with backup support).

**FR-DOC-04:** Documents are accessible only to users with permission to view the associated entity.

**FR-DOC-05:** Each document record stores: file name, file type, file size, upload date, and uploader.

**FR-DOC-06:** Documents can be downloaded but not edited within the system.

---

### 4.14 Digital Rent Agreement & E-Signature *(New — v1.3)*

This module enables the admin to send a digital rent agreement to the tenant for online review and e-signature before or at the time of lease activation. The digital agreement serves as an initial acceptance record. A separate government-approved notarized agreement is handled offline and uploaded into the system as a scanned document.

> **Legal disclaimer note to be displayed in the system UI:** *"This digital agreement is for initial acceptance only and does not replace a government-approved notarized rent agreement, which must be executed separately as per applicable law."*

---

**FR-ESIGN-01: Agreement Template Management**

The Super Admin and Property Manager can create and edit rent agreement templates. Only the Super Admin can delete templates. Each template contains:
- Template name (e.g., "Standard Residential Agreement", "Commercial Lease Agreement")
- Full agreement body (rich text, supporting paragraphs, numbered clauses, and bold/italic formatting)
- Dynamic placeholders that are auto-filled when an agreement is generated for a specific tenant and lease, including:
  - `{{tenant_name}}`, `{{tenant_address}}`, `{{property_name}}`, `{{unit_number}}`
  - `{{lease_start_date}}`, `{{lease_end_date}}`, `{{monthly_rent}}`, `{{security_deposit}}`
  - `{{landlord_name}}`, `{{agreement_date}}`
- Template status: Active / Inactive
- Created by and last modified by (with timestamps)

Multiple named templates can be maintained simultaneously. When generating an agreement for a tenant, the admin selects which template to use.

---

**FR-ESIGN-02: Agreement Generation**

When a new tenant is added or a lease is created, the system shall allow the admin or manager to generate a digital agreement for that tenant-lease combination:
- Select an active template from the list
- Review the auto-populated agreement preview with all placeholders resolved
- Make any manual edits to the generated content before sending (optional)
- Confirm and generate the agreement record

The generated agreement link has **no expiry** — it remains active and accessible until the tenant signs it or the agreement is manually voided by an admin or manager. This removes the need for tenants to act within a deadline and avoids the overhead of re-sending expired links.

Each generated agreement is assigned a unique token used in the shareable link. Only one active (unsigned) agreement can exist per lease at a time. If a new agreement is generated for the same lease, the previous unsigned agreement is automatically voided.

---

**FR-ESIGN-03: Shareable Agreement Link**

Upon generation, the system creates a unique, tokenised agreement URL in the format:

```
https://{domain}/agreement/{unique_token}
```

This link:
- Does not require the tenant to log into the portal — it is publicly accessible via the token
- Remains active indefinitely until the tenant signs or an admin voids the agreement
- Can be sent to the tenant via:
  - Email (system-generated, using the configured SMTP/email provider)
  - WhatsApp (via the configured SMS/WhatsApp provider)
  - Manual copy — the admin can copy the link and share via any channel
- The send action is logged (channel used, sent at timestamp, sent by)

The admin may resend the link or generate a new agreement link at any time if the tenant has not yet signed.

---

**FR-ESIGN-04: Tenant-Facing Agreement Page**

When the tenant opens the agreement link, they are shown a clean, mobile-friendly page (no login required) displaying:
- Property and unit details
- Lease period and rent amount
- Full agreement content (all clauses, formatted, scrollable)
- A scroll-to-bottom requirement — the "I Agree" checkbox is not enabled until the tenant has scrolled to the end of the agreement
- An **"I have read and agree to all the terms and conditions"** checkbox (mandatory)
- A digital signature panel supporting **draw signature only**:
  - **Draw** — tenant draws their freehand signature using a finger (on mobile/tablet) or mouse (on desktop) on a signature canvas. The canvas provides a Clear button to redo the signature. The drawn signature is captured as a PNG image upon submission.
- A **Submit** button

The Submit button is disabled until both the checkbox is selected AND a valid signature is provided.

---

**FR-ESIGN-05: Submission Validation**

The system shall enforce the following before accepting agreement submission:
- The "I Agree" checkbox must be checked
- A drawn signature must be present on the canvas (empty/blank canvas is rejected)
- The agreement must not already be in Signed status
- The agreement must not have been voided by an admin

If any condition fails, a clear inline error message is displayed and submission is blocked.

---

**FR-ESIGN-06: Post-Submission Processing**

Upon successful submission, the system shall:
1. Record the following signing metadata against the agreement:
   - Signing timestamp (date and time, IST)
   - IP address of the device used to sign
   - Device/browser user agent string
   - Signature image (PNG captured from the draw canvas)
   - Signing method: Draw
2. Mark the agreement status as **Signed**
3. Generate a final signed PDF document containing:
   - Full agreement content
   - Tenant's signature image embedded at the signature block
   - Signing metadata footer on the last page: *"Digitally signed by {tenant_name} on {date} at {time} from IP {ip_address}"*
4. Store the signed PDF in the document vault linked to both the tenant record and the lease record
5. Automatically send the signed PDF to the tenant via email as an attachment
6. Notify the manager and Super Admin that the agreement has been signed (see FR-NOTIF-01)

---

**FR-ESIGN-07: Agreement Status Tracking**

Each digital agreement record shall have one of the following statuses:

```
Draft → Sent → Viewed → Signed
                      → Voided (manually voided by admin/manager)
```

- **Draft:** Generated but link not yet sent to the tenant
- **Sent:** Link has been sent via at least one channel; tenant has not yet opened it
- **Viewed:** Tenant has opened the link at least once (tracked via first page load)
- **Signed:** Tenant has completed submission and signed PDF has been generated
- **Voided:** Manually voided by admin or manager, or superseded by a new agreement for the same lease

Status transitions are timestamped and logged in the audit trail.

---

**FR-ESIGN-08: Admin Agreement Dashboard**

From the tenant record and from the lease detail page, the admin and manager can see:
- Current agreement status with status badge (colour-coded)
- Date sent, date viewed (if applicable), date signed (if applicable)
- Which template was used
- Expiry date and time of the link (if applicable)
- Actions available based on status:
  - **Draft/Sent/Viewed:** Resend link, Copy link, Void agreement
  - **Signed:** Download signed PDF, View signing metadata
  - **Voided:** View voided agreement (read-only), Generate new agreement

---

**FR-ESIGN-09: Signed Agreement Storage & Access**

The signed PDF is stored in the document vault (Section 4.13) and linked at two levels:
- Tenant record → Documents tab → Agreement section
- Lease record → Documents tab → Signed Agreement

Access rules:
- Super Admin and Manager: can view and download
- Owner: can view agreement status only; cannot download signed PDF
- Tenant: can download their own signed agreement from the self-service portal

---

**FR-ESIGN-10: Notarized Agreement Upload**

After the offline government-approved notarized rent agreement is executed, the admin or manager can upload the scanned copy into the system:
- File formats accepted: PDF, JPG, PNG (max 10 MB)
- Upload is linked to the specific tenant record and lease
- Stored in the document vault under a dedicated "Notarized Agreement" category
- Upload date, uploaded by, and any notes are recorded
- The system displays a clear indicator on the tenant record showing whether the notarized agreement has been uploaded or is still pending

The notarized agreement upload is independent of the digital agreement signing workflow — both can exist simultaneously on the same lease.

---

**FR-ESIGN-11: Agreement Audit Trail**

The system shall maintain a full audit trail for every agreement record, capturing:
- Agreement generated (by whom, when, template used)
- Link sent (channel, sent by, sent at)
- Link opened (first viewed at, IP address)
- Submission attempted (if validation failed, reason logged)
- Agreement signed (timestamp, IP, device, method)
- PDF generated (timestamp, file reference)
- Email sent to tenant (timestamp, delivery status)
- Any admin actions (void, resend, download)

The audit trail is read-only and accessible to Super Admin only.

---

**FR-ESIGN-12: Agreement Completeness Indicator**

The tenant record and lease record shall display a clear agreement completeness indicator showing:
- Digital agreement status (Not Sent / Pending Signature / Signed / Voided)
- Notarized agreement status (Not Uploaded / Uploaded)

This gives managers a quick overview of documentation completeness without opening sub-sections.

### 5.1 Performance
- Page load time shall not exceed 3 seconds under normal load (up to 50 concurrent users).
- Document uploads shall support files up to 10 MB with progress indication.

### 5.2 Security
- All data transmitted over HTTPS (TLS 1.2+).
- Role-based access control enforced at both the UI and API/controller level.
- Sensitive files (KYC documents) accessible only via authenticated, time-limited signed URLs.
- All user actions on critical records (create, edit, delete, status change) are logged in an audit trail.
- Protection against OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities (SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, etc.) — Laravel's built-in protections shall be leveraged.
- Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) via Email OTP or WhatsApp OTP is mandatory for Super Admin and Property Manager on every login session (see Section 4.1a).
- OTPs are valid for 10 minutes and single-use. Accounts are locked after 5 consecutive failed OTP attempts.

### 5.3 Usability
- English language only.
- Responsive web design — usable on desktop and tablet browsers.
- Intuitive navigation with a sidebar-based layout.
- Form validations with clear inline error messages.

### 5.4 Reliability & Availability
- Target uptime: 99.5% (excluding scheduled maintenance).
- Daily automated database backups retained for 30 days.

### 5.5 Scalability
- Architecture must support scaling from 10 to 200+ properties without structural changes.
- Database schema must avoid hard-coded property counts or limits.

### 5.6 Maintainability
- Laravel MVC architecture with clean separation of concerns.
- Codebase documented with inline comments and a developer README.
- All configuration (notification thresholds, file size limits, etc.) in environment/config files, not hard-coded.

---

## 6. System Architecture

### 6.1 Technology Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|------------|
| Backend Framework | Laravel (PHP 8.x) |
| Frontend | Laravel Blade + Alpine.js / Livewire OR Vue.js (SPA) |
| Database | MySQL 8.x |
| File Storage | AWS S3 / Local disk (Laravel Filesystem abstraction) |
| Email | SMTP / Mailgun / SendGrid (via Laravel Mail) |
| SMS/WhatsApp | Twilio / MSG91 / Interakt (via Laravel Notifications) |
| Authentication | Laravel Sanctum / Fortify |
| Task Scheduling | Laravel Scheduler (for automated reminders) |
| Hosting | VPS / Shared hosting / Cloud (e.g., DigitalOcean, AWS EC2) |

### 6.2 High-Level Module Map

```
PMS
├── Auth & User Management
│   └── Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)  ← New in v1.4
├── Property Management
│   ├── Purchase Lifecycle
│   ├── Sales Lifecycle
│   └── Lifecycle Stage Tracker
├── Unit Management
├── Tenant & KYC Management
├── Lease Management
│   └── Security Deposit Management  ← New in v1.1
├── Rent & Payment Tracking
│   ├── Rent Ledger (monthly demand)  ← Revised in v1.2
│   ├── Instalment Recording          ← New in v1.2
│   └── Arrears Carry-Forward Engine  ← New in v1.2
├── Rent Return                        ← New in v1.5
├── Maintenance Requests
├── Co-Ownership Management
├── Financial Ledger & Reports
│   ├── Deposit Summary Report            ← New in v1.1
│   ├── Arrears & Partial Payments Report ← New in v1.2
│   └── Rent Return Report                ← New in v1.5
├── Document Vault
├── Digital Rent Agreement & E-Signature  ← New in v1.3
│   ├── Agreement Template Manager
│   ├── Agreement Generation & Link Engine
│   ├── Tenant Signing Portal (public, token-based)
│   ├── Signed PDF Generator
│   └── Notarized Agreement Upload
└── Notifications & Alerts Engine
```

---

## 7. Database Entities (High-Level)

| Entity | Key Attributes |
|--------|---------------|
| users | id, name, email, password, role, is_active |
| **user_2fa** *(New — v1.4)* | **id, user_id, preferred_method (email/whatsapp), is_configured, configured_at, last_verified_at, failed_attempts, locked_until, hard_lock** |
| **otp_tokens** *(New — v1.4)* | **id, user_id, otp_hash, channel, expires_at, used_at, login_session_id** |
| **backup_codes** *(New — v1.4)* | **id, user_id, code_hash, used_at, generated_at** |
| properties | id, name, type, address, lifecycle_stage, created_by |
| property_owners | id, property_id, user_id (or name), ownership_pct, capital_contribution |
| property_purchases | id, property_id, purchase_price, purchase_date, loan details |
| loan_emis | id, purchase_id, due_date, amount, paid_date, principal, interest, balance |
| property_sales | id, property_id, listing_date, asking_price, sale_price, sale_date |
| sale_leads | id, property_sale_id, buyer_name, offer_amount, status |
| units | id, property_id, unit_number, type, area, status, rent_amount |
| tenants | id, name, dob, phone, email, address, emergency_contact |
| tenant_documents | id, tenant_id, doc_type, file_path, uploaded_by |
| leases | id, unit_id, tenant_id, start_date, end_date, rent, deposit_amount, status |
| **lease_deposits** *(New — v1.1)* | **id, lease_id, amount_collected, collected_date, payment_mode, reference, status, recorded_by** |
| **deposit_deductions** *(New — v1.1)* | **id, lease_deposit_id, amount, category, reason, deduction_date, doc_path, recorded_by** |
| **deposit_refunds** *(New — v1.1)* | **id, lease_deposit_id, amount_refunded, refund_date, payment_mode, reference, notes, recorded_by** |
| ~~rent_payments~~ → **rent_ledger** *(Revised — v1.2)* | **id, lease_id, month, base_rent_due, carried_arrears, total_due, total_received, outstanding_balance, status, notes** |
| **rent_instalments** *(New — v1.2)* | **id, rent_ledger_id, instalment_no, amount_paid, payment_date, payment_mode, reference, late_fee, notes, recorded_by, voided, void_reason** |
| **lease_credits** *(New — v1.2)* | **id, lease_id, amount, source_month, applied_to_month, notes** |
| expenses | id, property_id, date, category, amount, vendor, receipt_path |
| maintenance_requests | id, unit_id, tenant_id, title, category, priority, status |
| documents | id, entity_type, entity_id, file_name, file_path, uploaded_by |
| notifications_log | id, user_id, event_type, channel, sent_at, status |
| audit_logs | id, user_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, timestamp |
| **agreement_templates** *(New — v1.3)* | **id, name, body_html, status, created_by, updated_by, created_at, updated_at** |
| **rent_agreements** *(New — v1.3)* | **id, lease_id, tenant_id, template_id, generated_content, token, status, expires_at, sent_at, sent_via, first_viewed_at, signed_at, signing_ip, signing_device, signing_method, signature_image_path, signed_pdf_path, voided_at, voided_by, generated_by** |
| **notarized_agreements** *(New — v1.3)* | **id, lease_id, tenant_id, file_path, upload_date, uploaded_by, notes** |
| **rent_returns** *(New — v1.5)* | **id, lease_id, tenant_id, unit_id, property_id, vacation_date, last_paid_through_date, billing_month, daily_rate, unused_days, suggested_amount, confirmed_amount, override_reason, status, settlement_method, settlement_date, settlement_reference, ledger_posted, ledger_entry_id, notes, initiated_by, initiated_at, processed_by, processed_at** |
